title: Bitcoin Mining Difficulty Surges 40% In 2024, Hits All-Time High
text: Bitcoin mining difficulty surged 40% in 2024, reaching a record-breaking 108.52 trillion on December 16. This latest adjustment marked a new all-time high, further underscoring the intense competition among Bitcoin miners and the growing resilience of the Bitcoin network. Difficulty Surge Drivers: Advancements in mining hardware, including more efficient ASIC miners that can perform more hashes per second with lower energy consumption, contributed to the increased competition. Additionally, as BTC prices rise, mining becomes more profitable, incentivizing more miners to deploy resources and secure the network. HUT 8's Position: Companies possessing stronger balance sheets and more efficient mining equipment, such as Hut 8, Bitdeer and Core Scientific, outperformed their peers. HUT 8 self-mined 89 BTC in December 2024, and the company was among the publicly traded Bitcoin miners successfully navigating the challenging post-halving environment. Market Dynamics: The rising difficulty in 2024 created significant competitive pressure, with smaller miners exiting the market due to tighter margins, while larger firms capitalized on M&A to scale operations and secure power access.
